_source: 00_REFERENCE_INTAKE/ssi/Large_Test_File_UID_152_Directional_Path_Analysis_2026-7-8-8-45-50.xlsx — SSI Directional Path Tool export, focus UID 152 (Large_Test_File.mpp 'USA OTB Master IMS', 2,126 tasks, progressed + leveled; delivered by the operator 2026-07-08, closes needs-list A-4).
_note: Golden SSI DRIVING PATH (Driving Slack <= 0 d) by UniqueID for the operator's large master IMS, focus UID 152. All 76 Path-01 tasks carry Driving Slack = 0 days; the engine reproduces the membership and every slack EXACTLY (test_ssi_driving_slack_uid152_exact). The export's Drag column is recorded for PROVENANCE ONLY and deliberately NOT gated: on this leveled file SSI reports 0 for milestones and for tasks whose stored window overlaps another on-path task, and a uniform 0.5 d for every serial task — the 0.5 is the near-path slack under an SSI convention the engine measures as 1.0 d (a half-day difference around a noon-milestone handoff). Gating engine drag to it without understanding that convention would be curve-fitting (Law 2); see ADR-0158.
_schedule_fixture: Large_Test_File.mspdi.xml.gz — MPXJ-converted MSPDI of the intake .mpp (gzipped; task names are anonymized strings in the source file itself).
